free_text: Double differential neutron emission cross sections
neutrons of 14.1 and 18.0 MeV at emission angles of
97.5 or 90. and 0 deg respectively, with an energy
dispersion of primary neutrons of about 300 keV FWHM.
NE213 scintillator, 14 cm diam and 10 cm thick,
Detector efficiency determined using Cf-252 fission
A second smaller NE213 scintillator monitored
size effect. The background due to parasitic and
degraded neutrons was estimated separately from
Monte-Carlo analysis and source neutron measurement.
Data from authors on tape by T.Nakagawa.
.EN-RSL added in common section of Subent 001
.Two references and several missing author names added
